query: Can you refer me to research that adapts the concept of Word Mover's Distance to sentences, addressing the limitations of bag-of-words approaches and considering the order of words for text similarity?
criteria: Word Mover's Distance sentence similarity word order
beyond bag-of-words text similarity word mover's distance
sentence-level document distance using word embeddings order

title.value: WMD O : Fluency-based Word Mover's Distance for Machine Translation Evaluation

recommendation_reason.value: Building on the Word Mover's Distance metric and various word embeddings, we introduce a fragmentation penalty to account for fluency of a translation.

supported_research_question.value: Matching in the semantic space has been investigated for translation evaluation, but the constraints of a translation's word order have not been fully explored.

method.value: we introduce a fragmentation penalty to account for fluency of a translation.

data_or_scenario.value: However, this requires linguistic resources to lemmatise the data or lexical databases to fetch synonyms/paraphrases, which do not exist for most languages.

main_conclusion.value: This word order extension is shown to perform better than standard WMD, with promising results against other types of metrics.

limitations.value: We propose WMD O , a metric based on distance between distributions in the semantic vector space.

relevance_strength.value: Building on the Word Mover's Distance metric and various word embeddings, we introduce a fragmentation penalty to account for fluency of a translation.

recommendation_reason.value: Word Mover's Distance is a textual distance metric which calculates the minimum transport cost between two sets of word embeddings.

supported_research_question.value: Word Mover's Distance is a textual distance metric which calculates the minimum transport cost between two sets of word embeddings.

method.value: Proceedings of the 7th Workshop on Representation Learning for NLP

data_or_scenario.value: This metric achieves impressive results on semantic similarity tasks, but is slow and difficult to scale due to the large number of floating point calculations.

main_conclusion.value: This metric achieves impressive results on semantic similarity tasks, but is slow and difficult to scale due to the large number of floating point calculations.

limitations.value: This metric achieves impressive results on semantic similarity tasks, but is slow and difficult to scale due to the large number of floating point calculations.

relevance_strength.value: Word Mover's Distance is a textual distance metric which calculates the minimum transport cost between two sets of word embeddings.

title.value: Sentence Mover's Similarity: Automatic Evaluation for Multi-Sentence Texts

recommendation_reason.value: We introduce methods based on sentence mover's similarity; our automatic metrics evaluate text in a continuous space using word and sentence embeddings.

supported_research_question.value: The most common automatic metrics, like BLEU and ROUGE, depend on exact word matching, an inflexible approach for measuring semantic similarity.

method.value: We introduce methods based on sentence mover's similarity

data_or_scenario.value: both on machine-generated summaries (average length of 3.4 sentences) and human-authored essays (average length of 7.5)

main_conclusion.value: We find that sentence-based metrics correlate with human judgments significantly better than ROUGE

limitations.value: However, existing automatic metrics for evaluating text are problematic.

relevance_strength.value: sentence mover's similarity can be used as a reward when learning a generation model via reinforcement learning
